orders.TechnicianComment:
Today we performed your rodent initial service.

We started by performing a post inspection in the 3 interior attic knee walls, removed old equipment, then placed new baited snap traps as necessary. We performed minor exclusion work by adding Xcluder steel wool to 3 sizable holes in the ground in the far right room with the 2 attic knee walls. We inspected and placed a baited snap trap down in the kitchen cabinet to the bottom right of the stove. We inspected and removed old equipment in both basements and replaced baited snap traps as necessary all throughout.

We also placed 2 large rodent bait stations on the exterior of the home along the right side of the property and in front of the porch

Please note while we were trapping throughout basement spaces, several areas of wood boring insect activity was observed. There was also evidence of termite activity in the crawlspace of the newer basement.

Thank you for choosing Catseye Pest Control and we hope you have a fantastic day!

orders.TechnicianComment: Today was your equipment service. I placed five exterior rodent monitoring stations throughout your home to help with rodent pressures. Thank you for choosing Catseye! Edwin

Today was your total platinum 1st year. Customer reported not hearing any activity in walls since last service. I inspected all rat traps in garage, attic in garage found no captures and traps were baited for possible rodent activity. I inspected all exterior rodent monitoring stations found no captures, no recent feeding to rodenticides and rodenticides one was replaced to monitor rodent activity. While inspecting the exterior I noticed wasp activity to soffit right of garage door and above ac condenser. I first applied a killing and flushing agent to both spots, didn’t see any wasp coming out and I then sealed off both wasp entryways with silicone. I applied a liquid residual treatment to the foundation, gable vents and soffits to help prevent crawling or flying insects activity. Thank you for choosing Catseye! Edwin

orders.TechnicianComment:
Good morning I conducted a full inspection of the basement snap traps and and bait devices I saw on dead mouse on the floor , Exclusion material was reinforced and redone where they chewed the plastic material around the vents to get it in I then placed fresh bait on snap traps and reset them in strategic locations to maximize effectiveness. 

 I also inspected all exterior rodent bait stations around the perimeter of the structure. Bait boxes were opened and checked for any signs of rodent feeding activity, damage, or tampering. 

Stations were found to be secure and in proper working condition at the time of inspection. Any debris inside the stations was cleared to ensure proper function.

Today we performed your termite soil treatment 

We started by trenching on the front and left side of the house and drilling every 12”  around driveway and all walkways along the right side and back side of the house. We then flooded the trench and flooded each drilled hole with a liquid termiticide. 

Due to an issue with the auger we will return to install bait stations. 

Thank you for choosing Catseye

orders.TechnicianComment:
Today was your Commercial Protection Program- Food service.
When I arrived I started in pizza station then bar then silver tanks and lounge and found a captured house mouse in the silver tank room and another captured house mouse in the lounge room, I extracted the captured mice and replaced the monitors.
I proceeded to inspect the concert hall and found a captured house mouse from behind the black speaker, I extracted the captured mouse and applied lure for freshness.
I then proceeded to inspect the kitchen and office stations and found no captures, I did find the mesh that I plugged an opening in the office pulled out, I plugged it again with more mesh to prevent any entry activity.
No other issues to report.
Thank you for choosing Cats Eye.

orders.OrderInstructions: 5/22 txt 5/27 CTG
orders.TechnicianComment:
Today was your Platinum Service x.
When I arrived I was greeted by the home owner who said that he had disposed of 3 rats in the basement.
I started in the attic and worked my way down to the basement and found no activity in the bait stations and no captures, I did installed 6 trex rat traps to capture incoming activity.
I then proceeded to inspect exterior perimeter for entrees and activity finding water damage to the soffits and bottom of the wood siding expanded away from the foundation both issues could be the reason the rodents are entering, upon inspection of the exterior stations I found 100% consumption of the bait, I replaced the bait and removed any debris.
Finally I applied a residual liquid barrier to protect against crawling insects.
No other issues to report.
Thank you for choosing Cats Eye.

orders.OrderInstructions: 5/22 txt 5/27 CTG
orders.TechnicianComment:
Good morning this is Alan with Catseye and today was your platinum service visit! Inside the home you commented on one juvenile rat catch by the garage door. I also had one adult rat catch by the sump pump. Rodent removed and fresh traps were set. Upstairs we had one house mouse catch in the stairway closet on the right as you go up the stairs and no other activity. On the exterior we had very heavy feeding at the back left and front left under the deck rodent stations. Very light feeding at the front right and the one up under the sitting area at the ramp. Fresh rodent bait set. Fly bait was applied near the catches to help minimize fly activity.

Dust has been applied to accessible cracks and crevices around the home. This does not kill immediately, but it should help you reduce numbers and prevent wasps from building their nests.  The liquid on your foundation should still be going strong. The scrap wood up at the top of the ramp has some carpenter rant attention. I went extremely heavy with some ant bait around the area and all the way down the ramp. Activity could be stirred up for a few days. A liquid product was used on the underside of the deck in accessible areas to help prevent wasp nest formation. Thank you for choosing Catseye. Have a wonderful afternoon!

orders.ServiceInstructions: **PHONE IS LANDLINE**
orders.TechnicianComment:
Good morning this is Alan with Catseye and today was your platinum service visit! In the basement we had feeding along a couple of the stations that follow the white pipe that starts when you get down the stairs. No mouse catches. We actually had one brown bat caught on my sticky trap at the corner to the right of the bulkhead. This is a strange one. It definitely means that there is a hole under your back deck, but I bet you should not be a regular visitor. No idea how it found its way in there. Moderate heavy, feeding and exterior rodent bait stations and fresh bait was set.

Couple of yellow jackets hanging out in the breezeway. I applied a little bit of dust to the inside of the windows since we have good ventilation and knocked a couple of them down with an aerosol product. These were big queens and not workers. They are still just getting started.  I also saw a lot of carpentry activity where the garage meets the main home. I injected foam into a couple of the bigger holes in the wooden sections near the garage where it touches the house. And bait was applied in a perimeter around the home and I went very heavy here. Liquid was also applied on the foundation of the home. None of these products work right away and could stir up an activity. Allow one to two weeks for full results. Dust has been applied along the eaves to help prevent wasps from getting established. Thank you for choosing Catseye. Have a wonderful afternoon!

orders.OrderInstructions: 5/22 txt 5/27 CTG
orders.TechnicianComment:
Good afternoon this is Alan with Catseye and today was your platinum service visit! Inside you had seen some small ants in the kitchen area. Ant bait has been applied in a perimeter around the kitchen. Do not be alarmed if this stirs up activity for a couple of days. On the exterior of the home, we had heavy feeding at each of the rodent bait stations. Fresh bait was set.

Ant bait has been applied around the exterior of the home. Ants are just coming out with the warm weather and they are hungry! Fresh ant bait could stir up activity in general around the home. Allow roughly one to two weeks for results. It’s going to be there waiting for them through to our next visit. 
A dust product has been applied around cracks and crevices of the home. This is not a true repellent, but bugs that touch it are on a timer! As the weather gets warmer, you are definitely going to see wasps! The point of this product is to help prevent them from building nests on the home.
 A liquid product has been applied around the foundation of the home. Again not a repellent, but bugs that touch it are on a timer. This is going to keep emerging pests at a minimum, and if ants start to track along the foundation, the slow reaction will take out the entire colony in a week or two. Thank you for choosing cats eye, and have a wonderful afternoon!

orders.TechnicianComment: Good afternoon this is Alan with Catseye in today. It was your rodent monitoring service visit with a focus on voles! Rodent stations on the right side of the house, had heavier feeding, but everything got chewed on at least halfway. You also commented on a juvenile wrap by your compost. Like a lot of mammals, rats have a breeding push in the early spring so this isn’t super weird. If you have any additional sightings of these, definitely let me know. You are already signed up for rodent monitoring, and if we start seeing Rats in the area, then I just set up a few extra boxes with a different flavor inside. Fresh rodent bait has been set all around. Thank you for choosing Catseye. Have a wonderful afternoon!

orders.OrderInstructions:
5/26 bf txt for 5/27 8-10 SAJ
5/26 bees nest by daughter's bedroom window, ants in home, wants ASAP SAJ
orders.TechnicianComment:
Today i arrived for your platinum service upon arrival i chis informed me he wasn’t seeing any mice activity inside.

I then applied some ant gel around the kitchen area I did let him know he will see a increase then a decrease in activity this is a normal procedure as they come get the bait and bring in back to the colony.

I then went to the exterior of the home and applied a liquid residual to help combat against crawling and flying insects including wasp bees hornets and ants.

I also treated around the tent area per request.

You might see the wasp being aggressive and agitated this will last 40 to 70hrs after treatment it is highly recommended to stay away from the areas treated. They will eventually die off or migrate away 

Thank you for choosing Catseye.

orders.TechnicianComment:
Today's your routine schedule treatment for the activities of rodents and insects 


The office 

I was told a mouse was sighted in the office. After inspected the perimeter I found droppings and one hole in back of the dress by the pipe in the wall. I sealed that opening with mesh and proceeded to change the monitoring boards in the traps has needed. The utility closet has shown no captures during today's treatments. The trap was reset and the rtu units shows no activity. After speaking with one of the workers the owner would like a cat guard or a door weather stop put at the bottom of the office door. As well as the utility closet door. 


The showroom floor 

I found one rodent in the trap. This could possibly be devoted from in the office. However I did dispose of the rodent and reset the traps. Monitoring boards have been switched and RT units show no activity. I noticed areas underneath the window sill that was previously blocked as gaps. They will need to be revealed. A perimeter spray for insect activity has been performed around the windowsill and both doors. No further treatment has been provided no exterior stations are at this location 


Thank you for choosing cat's eye we will continue to monitor and treat this location

orders.TechnicianComment:
55-3 

I inspected the unit for the activities of rodents. Underneath the sink I found an opening around the water pipe. I sold this opening with mesh. I didn't found debris of a phone behind the stove. The rodents have been trying to eat away at the phone behind the stove. I removed and replaced the monitoring boards. Also saw the activities of ants. I did a light perimeter spray for that ant activity. No activities. No sanitation issues..


55-2 


The tenant allows me to inspect the unit for the activities of rodents. However the kitchen is congested with boxes and bags of food and material. I've noticed food debris on the floor as well as the presence of fruit and drain flies. Food is left on the shells unattended. I recommend a follow-up. At this time prep should be made. 



55-1


This unit has shown signs of rodents. The son of the tenant has stated seeing a dead rodent in his closet. I informed the tenants that the area has to be prepped for treatment. There is no way I could hop on me treat this unit until prep is made. I found food debris dried up food as well as a large rotten apple behind the refrigerator. This unit has sanitation issues that need to be addressed in order to stop the activities of rodents. 


The basement 

The basement also has sanitation issues. All areas I congested and cluttered. All traps are dirty all monitor boards are useless. I replace the monitoring boards and the beat in the RT units. 



Exteriors 


I inspected all of the exterior devices and found light activity. During today's treatment I replenish the bait as needed. 



Prep has to be made in unit 2 and 1 for a proper treatment. Sanitation issues in both areas need to be addressed. 


Thank you for choosing cat's eye we will continue to monitor and treat this location

orders.TechnicianComment:
Good morning, today I performed your rodent plus initial service. Baited snap traps and glue boards have been placed in every room of the apartment besides bathrooms and your bedroom to capture any mice intruding the living space. I blocked a few areas off in the kitchen with steel wool to help prevent entry and it seems like mice are entering the unit through the walls or floor voids. 

Thank you for choosing Catseye!
-Kiefer

orders.TechnicianComment:
Good morning,

Today we completed your initial rodent service. My name is Dev, and I was your Catseye Pest Tech.

We performed a detailed inspection of the home and found that the attic areas, as mentioned in the report, appeared to be the main hot spots for rodent activity. Evidence of activity included rodent droppings, rub marks, and possible nesting areas. Additional inspection was completed throughout the basement, garage, and exterior to identify any conducive conditions and potential entry points contributing to the activity.

1. Installed rodent snap traps in both attic areas, basement, and garage to help control and monitor rodent activity. Traps were strategically placed along travel paths, corners, and secluded areas where activity was highest.

2. Installed additional snap traps inside the shed to help control and monitor any rodent activity present in that structure as well.

3. Applied a gel bait treatment in the downstairs bathroom to help control ant activity. Treatment was applied in targeted cracks and crevices where ants were trailing to reduce activity safely and effectively.

4. Installed exterior rodent bait stations around the perimeter of the home to help mitigate rodent activity outside and reduce the chances of rodents entering the structure. Stations were secured and placed in key areas conducive to rodent movement and harborage.

5. Sealed off a potential entry point leading toward the attic using durable steel mesh material to help prevent rodents from gaining access into the home. Additional vulnerable areas were inspected for future recommendations if needed.

Recommendations:
• Continue monitoring for any noises, droppings, or signs of rodent activity between visits.
• Store food products and pet food in sealed containers whenever possible.
• Reduce clutter in storage areas to limit hiding and nesting spots for rodents.
• Keep garage doors, shed doors, and exterior doors closed when not in use.
• Trim back any vegetation or tree branches touching or hanging close to the roofline.
• Avoid leaving standing water or accessible food sources around the home.

Expectations:
Rodent activity may increase slightly over the next 1–2 weeks as the traps and bait stations begin affecting the population. It is normal to hear or notice additional movement during this process. Consistent monitoring and follow-up services will help reduce activity over time. Full control may require multiple visits depending on the level of infestation and environmental conditions. Ant activity should begin decreasing within the next several days following treatment.

Thank you for choosing Catseye Pest Control. It was a pleasure servicing your home today. If you have any questions or notice any new activity before your next visit, please do not hesitate to contact us. Have a great day!

orders.TechnicianComment:
Good afternoon,

Today we completed your initial service on your home. A detailed inspection was performed throughout the interior and exterior of the property. During the inspection, we found several potential rodent entry points inside the garage area after vacuuming up rodent droppings and moving stored items to properly inspect hidden areas. Evidence of activity included droppings and openings around structural gaps that could allow rodents access into the home.

1. Sealed up the identified entry points using professional-grade silicone material to help prevent rodent entry into the garage and home. Sealing work was completed around gaps, cracks, and openings where activity or possible access points were discovered. This will help reduce the chances of rodents re-entering the structure moving forward.


2. Applied a liquid barrier treatment around the exterior perimeter of the home to help control crawling and nesting insect activity. Treatment was applied along the foundation, entry points, and other conducive areas to create a protective barrier against seasonal pests and insect intrusion.



During the exterior inspection, we observed a large amount of shrubs and vegetation positioned very close to the home, which limited visibility and accessibility in certain areas around the foundation. Dense vegetation can create conducive conditions for both rodents and insects by providing shelter, moisture retention, and hidden travel paths near the structure.

Recommendations:
• Trim back shrubs, bushes, and vegetation at least 12–18 inches away from the foundation to allow for proper inspection and treatment access.
• Continue monitoring for any signs of rodent activity such as droppings, noises, or gnaw marks.
• Keep storage items elevated and organized in the garage to reduce hiding areas for rodents.
• Store food products and pet food in sealed containers whenever possible.
• Seal any additional gaps or openings that may become visible after vegetation is cut back.
• Reduce moisture buildup around the home by maintaining gutters and downspouts properly.

Expectations:
You may continue to notice minor pest or rodent activity over the next couple of weeks as the treatment begins taking effect and rodents are displaced from nesting areas. The sealing work and exterior treatment will help reduce activity over time, but follow-up services and continued monitoring are important for long-term control. Cutting back vegetation will also greatly improve prevention efforts and future inspections.

Thank you for choosing Catseye Pest Control. It was a pleasure servicing your home today. If you have any questions or concerns before your next visit, please do not hesitate to contact us. Have a great day!

orders.TechnicianComment:
On today’s service visit upon arrival I met with the customer and she had advised me that there has been a dead rodent smell coming from downstairs.

I started off my inspection in the basement. 
There are two unfinished parts of the basement that are inspected in one of them have the boiler room.

There was a total of one dead deer mouse removed in the boiler room and a new trap was placed down to help continue monitoring anymore ongoing activity. 

There are multiple rat traps and regular mice snap traps that are in place and still intact with no other evidence of rodent activity found on today’s visit. 

Inspected the downstairs kitchen underneath the sink. There are no signs of any evidence of rodents. 

Inspected the upstairs kitchen. 
There are snap traps that were placed on top of the kitchen counter, as well as underneath the stove and sink that are in place and still intact. 

Inspected and serviced all scanned exterior IPM bait stations as needed. 
Only one of the exterior bait stations had minimum consumption of the rodenticide found and two new pieces of approved rodenticide were added to that one station.

Granular bait was treated alongside the soil foundation of the exterior of the house to help control, crawling insect activity, such as ants, crickets, spiders, and etc. 

Unfortunately, due to potential rain later on today and exterior spray foundation was not completed on today’s visit and will follow up next time. 

Nothing further to report. 

Thank you for choosing Catseye pest control to service you.

orders.TechnicianComment:
On today’s service visit upon arrival I met with the customers and they had advised me that no recent rodent activity has been seen and or found, but they are selling the house and have a potential buyer. They just wanted me to inspect early and remove any unnecessary glue boards and or snap trapped to not make it look like There is any type of rodent issue in the house.

I started off my inspection in the basement. 
Removed all old glue boards found, and remove some snap traps along the raptor of the basement. They are still snap traps in place and still attacked with no evidence of rodent activity found. 

Inspected the attic. 
Removed all glue boards found, and no concerning issues of any back and or rodent activity found. 

Inspected and serviced both exterior IPM bait stations as needed. 
High consumption of the road site was found in each device and two new pieces of approved rodenticide were added per station. 

Granular bait was treated alongside the soil foundation of the exterior of the house to help control, crawling insect activity, such as ants, crickets, spiders, and etc. 

And exterior spray foundation was treated out to the service visit on the pathway of the front entrance due to multiple ant hole scene.

Nothing further to report. 

Thank you for choosing Catseye pest control to service you.

orders.TechnicianComment:
Hello, today we began work on your lower cat-guard, by taking all necessary measurements and then fabricating metal to cover the gap between foundation and siding.

Installed white painted metal to match the trim of your home, with white painted concrete nails to the foundation and white painted 3/4” screws to the bottom of the siding where needed.

Started installation on right side of garage door and finished on opposite end of home, by a/c unit.

Tomorrow we will resume installation of metal to the foundation, then silicone all remaining gaps as needed.
We will replace your garage door gasket and install rodent guards to bottom corners on garage door, to protect weather stripping. 

We will also install a metal door sweep to the man-door to the basement inside bulkhead, which will complete your lower cat-guard. Then we will begin work on the upper areas of your home.

Cleaned all debris from site.
Thank you for choosing Catseye!

orders.TechnicianComment:
Good morning, today I performed your courtesy platinum service. I see that you have a tremendous amount of carpenter ants running throughout the entire house, but mostly centered at the first floor. I applied a liquid insecticide along the floors and ceilings of all rooms on the first and second floor to eliminate any ants inside the house. I then gel baited for the ants at baseboards of first floor and sprayed the exterior of the house. Thankfully no issues with the house itself but there is a lot of organic debris on the property close to the house (brush piles, leaf litter, etc) that I’m seeing a lot of carpenter ants inside. I highly recommend pulling this stuff as far from the house as possible and removing the vining plants from the top of the shed at the left side of the house. This will effectively remove the ants habitat and reduce the amount of ants that enter the house. No other issues identified. 

Thank you for choosing Catseye!
-Kiefer

orders.TechnicianComment:
Good afternoon, today I performed your courtesy platinum service. You showed me the bald faced hornets that have entered the house, and showed me where you suspect them to be entering in the basement. You also had concerns of mice entering the house so I placed a few mouse traps along the sills of the basement to capture any mice intruding the house but I’m not seeing any signs of recent activity. I blocked gaps in the windows to prevent any bees or rodents from entering, we also spoke about the necessity of nailing down and sealing the window flashing to prevent more from entering. I then applied a liquid insecticide to the entire foundation, around all windows and doors, gable vents, soffits, and the underside of the deck to prevent crawling or flying insects from entering / nesting on the house. This is a residual application that will remain effective for up to three weeks after today. No other issues identified. 

Thank you for choosing Catseye!
-Kiefer

orders.TechnicianComment:
On today’s service visit upon arrival I met with the customer and she had advised me that rodent droppings have been seen in the garage, occasional invader insect such as Beatles and wasp has been seen inside the house in the kitchen area and as well as downstairs in the basement.

I started off my inspection in the basement. 
Due to customer advise me that there was one dead mouse caught on a snap trap near the husband tools. 
Added a total of three new snap traps in this area to continue monitoring more ongoing rodent activity. 

Stare area that was a known hotspot was properly cleaned and sanitized really good by previous technician. 

Vacuumed the droppings in the garage. 
And properly sanitized with DSV hospital, graded sanitation in the corners of the garage as well as the son’s toy equipment. 

Exterior spray treatment was completed on today’s service visit with demand CS to help control any crawling insect activity, such as ants, crickets, Beatles, and etc. 

Nothing further to report. 

Thank you for choosing Catseye pest control to service you.

orders.TechnicianComment:
Hi, it’s Sean here. Thank you for letting me address your pest control needs. It was good to meet you & Mark today. When we spoke you described having captured 6 mice in snap traps below the sink recently. 

- Inspected kitchen area & observed mouse droppings beneath the oven & sink; refilled 2 mouse bait stations which were empty; reset snap traps as needed; used materials on hand to seal up gap running behind the cabinetry below the sink. Left dishwasher line open for monitoring. 

- Inspected basement & refilled mouse bait stations which were empty; reset snap traps as needed; used materials on hand to seal up interior bulkhead door frame leading to basement where there were indications of mice traveling. 

- Inspected the exterior of your home & located entry point on the foundation, right side. Loose siding on the front may be an issue. Crawlspace is not accessible at this time. 

- Inspected guest home & found no signs of any activity at this time. 

- Likely carpenter ant spotted entering floorboard gap by kitchen door leading to cement steps

 Please reach out with any questions or concerns & thank you for choosing USX.

orders.OrderInstructions: 5/22 notified for 5/27 JLM
orders.TechnicianComment:
We I arrived staff stated they did not remember I was coming. This has been an ongoing issue. I have wanted to service several units with a full clean out due to extreme levels of roach activity. I have been unable to complete a single clean out since we’ve started services due to preparation and not being prepared. This issue needs to be addressed immediately if we are to resolve any roach activity.

The following units were inspected.

2G, 3J, 3F, 3G, 3E

All show signs of heavy activity . Sanitation needs to improved throughout. Sanitation in 3F and 3G is absolutely disgusting. Open food and trash all over the floor. Floors are covered in a thick grime and are sticky to walk on. These two year to particular need serious work. 

I will be following up with my management to try to get a better system prior to arrival.

We will continue to monitor . Thank you for choosing Catseye

orders.TechnicianComment:
Good morning i performed a thorough inspection of the basement due to a history of moderate rodent activity. During the inspection I found heavy evidence of mice activity 

All existing rodent bait devices in the basement were inspected and serviced, and fresh bait was replaced as needed to ensure continued effectiveness. While inspecting the interior entry points associated with the basement, I identified a large opening around the dryer exhaust vent where it exits the home, which is a likely entry point for rodent activity. I then sealed it properly I also applied a liquid residual application around the foundation for crawling and flying insects.

I inspected all other areas with no activity i reset devices as necessary, i then inspected the upstairs which hasn’t had activity 
I then inspected the basement area, unfortunately a lot of our devices have been moved or covered by items. I inspected what was accessible I removed one dead rodent ( house mouse ) from the ketch all machine.

orders.TechnicianComment:
Good afternoon upon arrival of the property a complete inspection of the home was conducted for insect and rodent activity; the only activity I saw in the garage all rodenticides where changed and replaced bait into their designated locations. 

 I performed an exterior perimeter treatment targeting general crawling and flying insects. Inspected the property’s foundation, entry points, and surrounding landscape for insect activity. Applied a residual insecticide to the foundation, window frames eaves and other potential entry points. This will create a protective barrier for crawling and flying insects 

I Also performed a full inspection of all exterior rodent bait stations located around the perimeter of the home. Each station was checked for Activity,  moderate consumption of bait was observed I did fill them accordingly .

Next visit I will inspect termite stations thank you

orders.TechnicianComment:
Good morning this is Alan with Catseye today. Was your platinum service visit! My notes tell me I was doing the outside for you today. I also see that you were seeing some bees or wasps turning up dead in the basement. This is exactly what the dust is for in lousier weather on the sill, and much of the year around the exterior in general. Unless it’s something like roaches, dead bugs turning up is not the most attractive side effect, but it’s exactly what we are looking for. Heavy feeding at rodent stations and fresh rodent bait was set.

Ant bait has been applied around the exterior of the home. Ants are just coming out with the warm weather and they are hungry! Fresh ant bait could stir up activity in general around the home. Allow roughly one to two weeks for results. It’s going to be there waiting for them through to our next visit. 
A dust product has been applied around cracks and crevices of the home. This is not a true repellent, but bugs that touch it are on a timer! As the weather gets warmer, you are definitely going to see wasps! The point of this product is to help prevent them from building nests on the home.
 A liquid product has been applied around the foundation of the home. Again not a repellent, but bugs that touch it are on a timer. This is going to keep emerging pests at a minimum, and if ants start to track along the foundation, the slow reaction will take out the entire colony in a week or two. 

I also noticed a couple of yellow jackets hanging around the back deck. I flushed out some of the spaces between the deck slats on the edge with a flushing aerosol product. This also leaves some residual material behind to help prevent them from building nests. At this time of year, you are definitely going to see wasps around! My products should help keep them from making nests on the house.
Thank you for choosing cats eye, and have a wonderful afternoon!

orders.LocationInstructions:
++Landline
6 BUILDINGS EACH SERVICE 
12 buildings with 24 units per building a total of 288 units. Monthly services for rodents, ants, bees ,wasps etc. service will
include all 12 buildings, boiler rooms, Attics, exterior and up to five units upon request during routine scheduled
maintenance. On the interior will be using d-Tex nontoxic date for monitoring purposes. only switching to rodenticides if
activity is found. Interior stations are only to be inspected to replenish bait or if activity is reported. will not be going through
every building each service. If we are called out anytime other than a routine scheduled maintenance, it will be an additional
$99 per unit. Equipment will need to add 12 exterior stations in addition to using the other companies equipment which will
give us a total of four evos per building, 36 IQ stations, 120 snap traps.

orders.TechnicianComment:
Upon arrival, all interior equipment was inspected, rearmed, and rebaited as needed. A total of two rodent captures were found during today’s service visit. Additional equipment was deployed behind the pizzeria registers in order to help intercept further activity.

A liquid residual treatment was then applied throughout the kitchen area and all three bar areas. From there, service continued down to the basement level where all interior equipment was inspected. No rodent captures or signs of new activity were found within the basement area.

We will continue to monitor. Thank you for choosing Catseye pest control services.

orders.TechnicianComment: Today we provided your regularly scheduled pest control service. Today we inspected all interior rodent stations for activity heavy activity, discovered in dining and bar area seven captures found no activity discovered in Kitchen area today we inspected front interior wall for rodent entry point no entry points had been discovered on interior however upon inspecting exterior front wall by outdoor dining area, two large holes discovered at base of wall below overhang holes had been sealed with exclusion material to help restrict entry into structure. also several small holes discovered around pipes along back of building all holes sealed with exclusion material. This should help to restrict Ronan entry in these areas, however upon inspecting front entry area around dining patio, discovered front door had not been fully shut the night before door had gap in it, allowing rodent access into structure. I highly recommend checking doors at the end of every shift to  secure door.  Also, today we installed an additional exterior rodent station at front left-hand corner of building behind shrubs, located nearby bicycle rack. This will help to reduce rodent activity around structure.        if you have any questions or concerns related to today’s service, please contact our office and will return your call as soon as possible thank you for choosing Catseye pest services.

orders.TechnicianComment:
Good afternoon, today I performed your rodent plus follow up service. You reported that one mouse was caught and removed from the basement electric panel area. I placed additional traps in the basement for continued monitoring and didn’t find any additional captures here or in the attic. I then checked the exterior to find about a dozen large holes in the siding, foundation, and around utility penetrations at the front of the house. All openings have been packed with steel wool to prevent additional mice from entering the house. Due to the size of these gaps (one was almost fist sized) I have no doubt these were main entrances, but will inspect the remaining foundation during next weeks follow up visit. No other issues identified. 

Thank you for choosing Catseye!
-Kiefer

Customer has purchased a Termite Defense System with soil treatment and monitoring. If activity is found in the monitoring cartridge, replace that cartridge with an active termiticide cartridge and notate account

During April-May and August-September services, technician MUST perform a full interior/exterior inspection for termite activity. Technician must also service all termite monitors installed on premises. Termite monitors must be inspected/serviced at least 2 times per year.

orders.TechnicianComment:
Today I arrived and spoke to the tenant in Unit 28 who had no inside issues.  Then I went to Unit 30 and checked all areas and devices for mice. No activity found. After that I went outside and inspected and rebaited,relabeled all the exterior rodent stations as needed.  Mostly activity on the stations out front.
Then I added 2 mice bait stations inside the detached garage.  No other issues. Thank you for choosing Catseye and have a great day. Paul

orders.TechnicianComment:
Hello,

   My name is Christian and I completed platinum services for your home today. I checked the attic first and found a couple of wasps hanging out. I got rid of them and used an aerosol on most eaves towards the soffits to hopefully get rid of any possible starter nests. I found slight basement activity but no activity elsewhere for mice. I checked exterior bait stations and found light to moderate activity and bait needed to be replaced in one. I did not treat the exterior foundation today as the last product we used has a long lasting residual (3 months or more) and can take up to 3 months to fully do its job. Treating over it can ruin the initial treatment so I left it as is. You also said ants have been low in number of any at all.

   Thank you for choosing Catseye Pest Control and have a great day!

orders.TechnicianComment:
Good afternoon,

Today we completed your rodent follow-up service. Upon arrival, we found 3 rodent captures, which were safely removed from the home, and all traps were reset to continue monitoring and controlling activity. A detailed inspection was also completed throughout the previously affected areas to check for any new signs of rodent movement, entry points, or conducive conditions.

1. Vacuumed up the majority of the accessible rodent droppings throughout the home to help reduce contamination and improve sanitation conditions. Special attention was given to the basement, storage areas, attic access points, and other areas where activity had previously been noted. Areas that were inaccessible or obstructed will continue to be monitored during future visits.


2. Sanitized all areas that were vacuumed to help eliminate bacteria, odors, and contaminants associated with rodent activity. Treatment was applied throughout the affected spaces to help create a cleaner and safer environment moving forward.



Recommendations:
• Continue monitoring for any signs of rodent activity such as droppings, noises, or damaged materials.
• Keep food products sealed and limit accessible food and water sources.
• Reduce clutter in storage areas to minimize rodent hiding and nesting areas.
• Ensure garage doors and exterior doors remain closed when not in use.
• Inform us if any new activity is noticed between scheduled visits.

Expectations:
It is normal to continue seeing occasional rodent activity during the treatment process as traps remain active and rodents are displaced from nesting areas. Activity should gradually decline with continued servicing and monitoring. Follow-up visits are important to ensure the rodent population is fully controlled and to address any additional problem areas that may arise.

Thank you for choosing Catseye Pest Control. We appreciate the opportunity to service your home today. If you have any questions or concerns before the next visit, please feel free to contact us. Have a great day!

orders.TechnicianComment:
On today’s service visit upon arrival, John and I met with a customer and she had advised us at only rodent activity scene was one dead mouse near the pool.

John treated the attic with Talstar in his b&g

Inspected and serviced all exterior IPM bait stations as needed. 
Minimum consumption of the road was found, and the one station with the consumption was near the pool pump. 
Replaced with two pieces of approved rodenticide. 

Delta dust injection was treated in the crack & crevices of the exterior of the house to help control, crawling insect activity, such as ants, crickets, spiders, and etc. 

Granular bait was treated alongside the soil foundation of the exterior of the house to also help control with crawling insect activity. 

With my web removal, I passed it alongside the corners of the overhead garage, side, back & main door entrance to remove any visible cobwebs. 

Exterior spray foundation was completed on today’s service visit.

Inspected the basement. 
None of the RTU had any consumption of the rodenticide found. 
Placed down supplementary insect monitor and glue board in the unfinished part of the basement to monitor and control any crawling insect activity. 
No concern concerning issues of rodent activity were found. 

Nothing further to report. 

Thank you for choosing Catseye pest control to service you.

orders.TechnicianComment:
Hi, it’s Sean. Thank you for letting me address your pest control needs. It was good to see you today. When we spoke you described capturing a few mice since the last visit, in the kitchen, basement, & 2nd floor bathroom. 

- Removed 1 mouse captured in a snap trap behind the couch, & 1 in the basement. Reset all snap traps & applied fresh bait as needed. There was no new consumption of bait in bait stations. 

- Inspected & refilled 2 exterior rodent bait stations; consumption was minimal at this time. 

- Applied a residual synthetic liquid insecticide to the exterior foundation, window & door trim, eaves & soffit panels to combat a broad spectrum of insects & spiders. 

 Please reach out with any questions or concerns, & thank you for choosing USX!

orders.LocationInstructions: This agreement is for the Commercial Protection Program for Rodent Monitoring services including the installation of exterior bait and monitoring equipment throughout the grounds. Roughly 35 stations will be installed, tucked under units and around the perimeter of the main house and lodge. We will return two times a month to inspect, re-bait, and maintain the equipment. If insect related applications or one time treatments are necessary or requested, they will come at a rate of $199 per service unless stated otherwise prior to servicing. Bed Bugs are not included in this service and will be priced individually on a per case basis.

orders.ServiceInstructions:
Today I performed your initial rodent plus service for mice. I installed a total of 42 snap traps - 1st floor- 2 kitchen stove, 3 bedroom and 3 dinning room -basement 20 and 12 snap traps on the 3rd floor. Nobody home on 2nd floor will set traps and stations next visit. Added 2 bait stations to first floor and 4 bait stations to the basement. Also set up 3 exterior bait stations around perimeter of the building. Very minimal mice activity noticed. Will return to check all traps, bait stations and remove any mice caught. Thank you for using Catseye.

Set up aggressive trapping program throughout the basement and all 3 units. Seal minor gaps on the exterior on all sides of home (gaps observed on the back side near deck). Seals gaps in the kitchen of unit 1 as well as around radiators in the dining room.

Provide 6 follow ups for house mice to check, reset, remove dead rodents and allocate traps to areas of higher activity. HEPA vac droppings and disinfect areas (hard surfaces only) at the last follow up.

Set up bi-monthly home protection plan thereafter.
